reasoning_model_1: The model generates the audio content '关键在于沟通，我们必须保持开放。' with perfect content accuracy; there are no mispronunciations or omissions. In terms of fluency (0.0s - 3.2s), the speech is smooth and natural, lacking mechanical artifacts. However, regarding the specific paralinguistic command to emphasize '关键' (Key), the model falls short. The segment '关键' (0.0s - 0.5s) is delivered with a standard, flat declarative intonation that matches the energy of the rest of the sentence. There is no discernible increase in pitch, intensity, or duration to highlight this word, nor is there a prosodic pause following it. Consequently, while the audio is high-quality speech, it fails to execute the user's specific control instruction.
reasoning_model_2: The model accurately reproduces the script '关键在于沟通，我们必须保持开放。' with high fidelity and naturalness. The fluency is excellent, indistinguishable from human speech. Crucially, the model successfully adheres to the paralinguistic instruction to emphasize '关键'. Analyzing the segment from 0.0s to 0.7s, the word '关键' is pronounced with noticeably higher intensity and a slight elongation compared to the subsequent text. Furthermore, there is a very brief, natural micropause (around 0.7s - 0.8s) before '在于沟通', which effectively isolates and highlights the keyword. This prosodic variation correctly conveys the intended stress and makes the key point stand out vividly.
model_comparison: Both models exhibit excellent performance in terms of content accuracy and general fluency; neither produced mechanical artifacts or misreadings. The comparison hinges entirely on the 'Paralinguistic Compliance' dimension regarding the command to emphasize the word '关键'. Model 1 provided a neutral, baseline reading where the keyword was indistinguishable in tone from the surrounding text. Model 2, in contrast, demonstrated superior control by applying appropriate stress (increased duration and intensity) to '关键' and utilizing a subtle pause to reinforce the emphasis. Model 2 successfully fulfilled the specific feature control requirement, whereas Model 1 ignored it.
